ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: By those [angels] lined up in rows
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: By those ˹angels˺ lined up in ranks,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who drive [the clouds]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and those who diligently drive ˹the clouds˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who recite the message,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and those who recite the Reminder!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, your God is One,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely your God is One!

ayahs.textArabic: رَّبُّ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا وَرَبُّ ٱلۡمَشَٰرِقِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Rabbu alssamawati waalardi wama baynahuma warabbu almashariqi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Lord of the heavens and the earth and that between them and Lord of the sunrises.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹He is˺ the Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between, and the Lord of all points of sunrise.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّا زَيَّنَّا ٱلسَّمَآءَ ٱلدُّنۡيَا بِزِينَةٍ ٱلۡكَوَاكِبِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Inna zayyanna alssamaa alddunya bizeenatin alkawakibi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We have adorned the nearest heaven with an adornment of stars
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, We have adorned the lowest heaven with the stars for decoration

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And as protection against every rebellious devil
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and ˹for˺ protection from every rebellious devil.

ayahs.textArabic: لَّا يَسَّمَّعُونَ إِلَى ٱلۡمَلَإِ ٱلۡأَعۡلَىٰ وَيُقۡذَفُونَ مِن كُلِّ جَانِبٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: La yassammaAAoona ila almalai alaAAla wayuqthafoona min kulli janibin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [So] they may not listen to the exalted assembly [of angels] and are pelted from every side,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They cannot listen to the highest assembly ˹of angels˺ for they are pelted from every side,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Repelled; and for them is a constant punishment,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹fiercely˺ driven away. And they will suffer an everlasting torment.

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا مَنۡ خَطِفَ ٱلۡخَطۡفَةَ فَأَتۡبَعَهُۥ شِهَابٞ ثَاقِبٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa man khatifa alkhatfata faatbaAAahu shihabun thaqibun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except one who snatches [some words] by theft, but they are pursued by a burning flame, piercing [in brightness].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But whoever manages to stealthily eavesdrop is ˹instantly˺ pursued by a piercing flare.

ayahs.textArabic: فَٱسۡتَفۡتِهِمۡ أَهُمۡ أَشَدُّ خَلۡقًا أَم مَّنۡ خَلَقۡنَآۚ إِنَّا خَلَقۡنَٰهُم مِّن طِينٖ لَّازِبِۭ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faistaftihim ahum ashaddu khalqan am man khalaqna inna khalaqnahum min teenin lazibin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then inquire of them, [O Muhammad], "Are they a stronger [or more difficult] creation or those [others] We have created?" Indeed, We created men from sticky clay.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So ask them ˹O Prophet˺, which is harder to create: them or other marvels of Our creation? Indeed, We created them from a sticky clay.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But you wonder, while they mock,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, you are astonished ˹by their denial˺, while they ridicule ˹you˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when they are reminded, they remember not.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When they are reminded, they are never mindful.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when they see a sign, they ridicule
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And whenever they see a sign, they make fun of it,

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالُوٓاْ إِنۡ هَٰذَآ إِلَّا سِحۡرٞ مُّبِينٌ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqaloo in hatha illa sihrun mubeenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And say, "This is not but obvious magic.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: saying, “This is nothing but pure magic.

ayahs.textArabic: أَءِذَا مِتۡنَا وَكُنَّا تُرَابٗا وَعِظَٰمًا أَءِنَّا لَمَبۡعُوثُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Aitha mitna wakunna turaban waAAithaman ainna lamabAAoothoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When we have died and become dust and bones, are we indeed to be resurrected?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When we are dead and reduced to dust and bones, will we really be resurrected?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And our forefathers [as well]?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And our forefathers as well?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "Yes, and you will be [rendered] contemptible."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, “Yes! And you will be fully humbled.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَإِنَّمَا هِيَ زَجۡرَةٞ وَٰحِدَةٞ فَإِذَا هُمۡ يَنظُرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fainnama hiya zajratun wahidatun faitha hum yanthuroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: It will be only one shout, and at once they will be observing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It will only take one Blast, then at once they will see ˹it all˺.

ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqaloo ya waylana hatha yawmu alddeeni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will say, "O woe to us! This is the Day of Recompense."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will cry, “Oh, woe to us! This is the Day of Judgment!”

ayahs.textArabic: هَٰذَا يَوۡمُ ٱلۡفَصۡلِ ٱلَّذِي كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hatha yawmu alfasli allathee kuntum bihi tukaththiboona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [They will be told], "This is the Day of Judgement which you used to deny."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They will be told,˺ “This is the Day of ˹Final˺ Decision which you used to deny.”

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ ٱحۡشُرُواْ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُواْ وَأَزۡوَٰجَهُمۡ وَمَا كَانُواْ يَعۡبُدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ohshuroo allatheena thalamoo waazwajahum wama kanoo yaAAbudoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [The angels will be ordered], "Gather those who committed wrong, their kinds, and what they used to worship
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Allah will say to the angels,˺ “Gather ˹all˺ the wrongdoers along with their peers, and whatever they used to worship

ayahs.textArabic: مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ فَٱهۡدُوهُمۡ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطِ ٱلۡجَحِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Min dooni Allahi faihdoohum ila sirati aljaheemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Other than Allah, and guide them to the path of Hellfire
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: instead of Allah, then lead them ˹all˺ to the path of Hell.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And stop them; indeed, they are to be questioned."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And detain them, for they must be questioned.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [They will be asked], "What is [wrong] with you? Why do you not help each other?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Then they will be asked,˺ “What is the matter with you that you can no longer help each other?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But they, that Day, are in surrender.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, on that Day they will be ˹fully˺ submissive.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they will approach one another blaming each other.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will turn on each other, throwing blame.

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُوٓاْ إِنَّكُمۡ كُنتُمۡ تَأۡتُونَنَا عَنِ ٱلۡيَمِينِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo innakum kuntum tatoonana AAani alyameeni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will say, "Indeed, you used to come at us from the right."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The misled will say, “It was you who deluded us away from what is right.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The oppressors will say, "Rather, you [yourselves] were not believers,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The misleaders will reply, “No! You disbelieved on your own.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا كَانَ لَنَا عَلَيۡكُم مِّن سُلۡطَٰنِۭۖ بَلۡ كُنتُمۡ قَوۡمٗا طَٰغِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama kana lana AAalaykum min sultanin bal kuntum qawman tagheena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And we had over you no authority, but you were a transgressing people.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We had no authority over you. In fact, you yourselves were a transgressing people.

ayahs.textArabic: فَحَقَّ عَلَيۡنَا قَوۡلُ رَبِّنَآۖ إِنَّا لَذَآئِقُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fahaqqa AAalayna qawlu rabbina inna lathaiqoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So the word of our Lord has come into effect upon us; indeed, we will taste [punishment].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The decree of our Lord has come to pass against us ˹all˺: we will certainly taste ˹the punishment˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And we led you to deviation; indeed, we were deviators."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We caused you to deviate, for we ourselves were deviant.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So indeed they, that Day, will be sharing in the punishment.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely on that Day they will ˹all˺ share in the punishment.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, that is how We deal with the criminals.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: That is certainly how We deal with the wicked.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّهُمۡ كَانُوٓاْ إِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمۡ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ يَسۡتَكۡبِرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innahum kanoo itha qeela lahum la ilaha illa Allahu yastakbiroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed they, when it was said to them, "There is no deity but Allah," were arrogant
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: For whenever it was said to them ˹in the world˺, “There is no god ˹worthy of worship˺ except Allah,” they acted arrogantly

ayahs.textArabic: وَيَقُولُونَ أَئِنَّا لَتَارِكُوٓاْ ءَالِهَتِنَا لِشَاعِرٖ مَّجۡنُونِۭ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wayaqooloona ainna latarikoo alihatina lishaAAirin majnoonin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And were saying, "Are we to leave our gods for a mad poet?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and argued, “Should we really abandon our gods for a mad poet?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Rather, the Prophet has come with the truth and confirmed the [previous] messengers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, he came with the truth, confirming ˹earlier˺ messengers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, you [disbelievers] will be tasters of the painful punishment,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: You will certainly taste the painful torment,

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا تُجۡزَوۡنَ إِلَّا مَا كُنتُمۡ تَعۡمَلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama tujzawna illa ma kuntum taAAmaloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And you will not be recompensed except for what you used to do -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and will only be rewarded for what you used to do.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But not the chosen servants of Allah.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But not the chosen servants of Allah.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Those will have a provision determined -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will have a known provision:

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Fruits; and they will be honored
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: fruits ˹of every type˺. And they will be honoured

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: In gardens of pleasure
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: in the Gardens of Bliss,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: On thrones facing one another.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: facing each other on thrones.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: There will be circulated among them a cup [of wine] from a flowing spring,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: A drink ˹of pure wine˺ will be passed around to them from a flowing stream:

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: White and delicious to the drinkers;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: crystal-white, delicious to drink.

ayahs.textArabic: لَا فِيهَا غَوۡلٞ وَلَا هُمۡ عَنۡهَا يُنزَفُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: La feeha ghawlun wala hum AAanha yunzafoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No bad effect is there in it, nor from it will they be intoxicated.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It will neither harm ˹them˺, nor will they be intoxicated by it.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And with them will be women limiting [their] glances, with large, [beautiful] eyes,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And with them will be maidens of modest gaze and gorgeous eyes,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: As if they were [delicate] eggs, well-protected.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: as if they were pristine pearls.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they will approach one another, inquiring of each other.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then they will turn to one another inquisitively.

ayahs.textArabic: قَالَ قَآئِلٞ مِّنۡهُمۡ إِنِّي كَانَ لِي قَرِينٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qala qailun minhum innee kana lee qareenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: A speaker among them will say, "Indeed, I had a companion [on earth]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: One of them will say, “I once had a companion ˹in the world˺

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Who would say, 'Are you indeed of those who believe
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: who used to ask ˹me˺, ‘Do you actually believe ˹in resurrection˺?

ayahs.textArabic: أَءِذَا مِتۡنَا وَكُنَّا تُرَابٗا وَعِظَٰمًا أَءِنَّا لَمَدِينُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Aitha mitna wakunna turaban waAAithaman ainna lamadeenoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That when we have died and become dust and bones, we will indeed be recompensed?'"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When we are dead and reduced to dust and bones, will we really be brought to judgment?’”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He will say, "Would you [care to] look?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He will ˹then˺ ask, “Would you care to see ˹his fate˺?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he will look and see him in the midst of the Hellfire.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then he ˹and the others˺ will look and spot him in the midst of the Hellfire.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He will say, "By Allah, you almost ruined me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He will ˹then˺ say, “By Allah! You nearly ruined me.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَوۡلَا نِعۡمَةُ رَبِّي لَكُنتُ مِنَ ٱلۡمُحۡضَرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walawla niAAmatu rabbee lakuntu mina almuhdareena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: If not for the favor of my Lord, I would have been of those brought in [to Hell].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Had it not been for the grace of my Lord, I ˹too˺ would have certainly been among those brought ˹to Hell˺.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then, are we not to die
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Then he will ask his fellow believers,˺ “Can you imagine that we will never die,

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا مَوۡتَتَنَا ٱلۡأُولَىٰ وَمَا نَحۡنُ بِمُعَذَّبِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa mawtatana aloola wama nahnu bimuAAaththabeena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except for our first death, and we will not be punished?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: except our first death, nor be punished ˹like the others˺?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, this is the great attainment.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This is truly the ultimate triumph.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: For the like of this let the workers [on earth] work.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: For such ˹honour˺ all should strive.

ayahs.textArabic: أَذَٰلِكَ خَيۡرٞ نُّزُلًا أَمۡ شَجَرَةُ ٱلزَّقُّومِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Athalika khayrun nuzulan am shajaratu alzzaqqoomi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Is Paradise a better accommodation or the tree of zaqqum?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Is this ˹bliss˺ a better accommodation or the tree of Zaqqûm?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We have made it a torment for the wrongdoers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We have surely made it a test for the wrongdoers.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّهَا شَجَرَةٞ تَخۡرُجُ فِيٓ أَصۡلِ ٱلۡجَحِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innaha shajaratun takhruju fee asli aljaheemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, it is a tree issuing from the bottom of the Hellfire,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, it is a tree that grows in the depths of Hell,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Its emerging fruit as if it was heads of the devils.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: bearing fruit like devils’ heads.

ayahs.textArabic: فَإِنَّهُمۡ لَأٓكِلُونَ مِنۡهَا فَمَالِـُٔونَ مِنۡهَا ٱلۡبُطُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fainnahum laakiloona minha famalioona minha albutoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, they will eat from it and fill with it their bellies.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The evildoers will certainly ˹be left to˺ eat from it, filling up their bellies with it.

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ إِنَّ لَهُمۡ عَلَيۡهَا لَشَوۡبٗا مِّنۡ حَمِيمٖ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma inna lahum AAalayha lashawban min hameemin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then indeed, they will have after it a mixture of scalding water.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then on top of that they will be given a blend of boiling drink.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then indeed, their return will be to the Hellfire.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then they will ultimately return to ˹their place in˺ Hell.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed they found their fathers astray.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, they found their forefathers astray,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So they hastened [to follow] in their footsteps.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: so they rushed in their footsteps!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And there had already strayed before them most of the former peoples,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And surely most of the earlier generations had strayed before them,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We had already sent among them warners.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: although We had certainly sent warners among them.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then look how was the end of those who were warned -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: See then what was the end of those who had been warned.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But not the chosen servants of Allah.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But not the chosen servants of Allah.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And Noah had certainly called Us, and [We are] the best of responders.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, Noah cried out to Us, and how excellent are We in responding!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We saved him and his family from the great affliction.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We delivered him and his family from the great distress,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We made his descendants those remaining [on the earth]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and made his descendants the sole survivors.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And left for him [favorable mention] among later generations:
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We blessed him ˹with honourable mention˺ among later generations:

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: "Peace upon Noah among the worlds."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: “Peace be upon Noah among all peoples.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, this is how We reward the good-doers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, he was of Our believing servants.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹For˺ he was truly one of Our faithful servants.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We drowned the disbelievers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then We drowned the others.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, among his kind was Abraham,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And indeed, one of those who followed his way was Abraham.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When he came to his Lord with a sound heart
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Remember˺ when he came to his Lord with a pure heart,

ayahs.textArabic: إِذۡ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوۡمِهِۦ مَاذَا تَعۡبُدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ith qala liabeehi waqawmihi matha taAAbudoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [And] when he said to his father and his people, "What do you worship?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and said to his father and his people, “What are you worshipping?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Is it falsehood [as] gods other than Allah you desire?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Is it false gods that you desire instead of Allah?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then what is your thought about the Lord of the worlds?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: What then do you expect from the Lord of all worlds?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he cast a look at the stars
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He later looked up to the stars ˹in contemplation˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And said, "Indeed, I am [about to be] ill."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then said, “I am really sick.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So they turned away from him, departing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So they turned their backs on him and went away.

ayahs.textArabic: فَرَاغَ إِلَىٰٓ ءَالِهَتِهِمۡ فَقَالَ أَلَا تَأۡكُلُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faragha ila alihatihim faqala ala takuloona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then he turned to their gods and said, "Do you not eat?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then he ˹stealthily˺ advanced towards their gods, and said ˹mockingly˺, “Will you not eat ˹your offerings˺?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: What is [wrong] with you that you do not speak?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: What is wrong with you that you cannot speak?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he turned upon them a blow with [his] right hand.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then he swiftly turned on them, striking ˹them˺ with his right hand.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then the people came toward him, hastening.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Later, his people came rushing towards him ˹furiously˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He said, "Do you worship that which you [yourselves] carve,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He argued, “How can you worship what you carve ˹with your own hands˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: While Allah created you and that which you do?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: when it is Allah Who created you and whatever you do?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ ٱبۡنُواْ لَهُۥ بُنۡيَٰنٗا فَأَلۡقُوهُ فِي ٱلۡجَحِيمِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo ibnoo lahu bunyanan faalqoohu fee aljaheemi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They said, "Construct for him a furnace and throw him into the burning fire."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They said ˹to one another˺, “Build him a furnace and cast him into the blazing fire.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they intended for him a plan, but We made them the most debased.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And so they sought to harm him, but We made them inferior.

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالَ إِنِّي ذَاهِبٌ إِلَىٰ رَبِّي سَيَهۡدِينِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqala innee thahibun ila rabbee sayahdeeni
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [then] he said, "Indeed, I will go to [where I am ordered by] my Lord; He will guide me.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He later said, “I am leaving ˹in obedience˺ to my Lord. He will guide me.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: My Lord, grant me [a child] from among the righteous."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: My Lord! Bless me with righteous offspring.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So We gave him good tidings of a forbearing boy.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So We gave him good news of a forbearing son.

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَمَّا بَلَغَ مَعَهُ ٱلسَّعۡيَ قَالَ يَٰبُنَيَّ إِنِّيٓ أَرَىٰ فِي ٱلۡمَنَامِ أَنِّيٓ أَذۡبَحُكَ فَٱنظُرۡ مَاذَا تَرَىٰۚ قَالَ يَٰٓأَبَتِ ٱفۡعَلۡ مَا تُؤۡمَرُۖ سَتَجِدُنِيٓ إِن شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ مِنَ ٱلصَّٰبِرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Falamma balagha maAAahu alssaAAya qala ya bunayya innee ara fee almanami annee athbahuka faonthur matha tara qala ya abati ifAAal ma tumaru satajidunee in shaa Allahu mina alssabireena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when he reached with him [the age of] exertion, he said, "O my son, indeed I have seen in a dream that I [must] sacrifice you, so see what you think." He said, "O my father, do as you are commanded. You will find me, if Allah wills, of the steadfast."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then when the boy reached the age to work with him, Abraham said, “O my dear son! I have seen in a dream that I ˹must˺ sacrifice you. So tell me what you think.” He replied, “O my dear father! Do as you are commanded. Allah willing, you will find me steadfast.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when they had both submitted and he put him down upon his forehead,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then when they submitted ˹to Allah’s Will˺, and Abraham laid him on his forehead ˹for sacrifice˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We called to him, "O Abraham,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We called out to him, “O Abraham!

ayahs.textArabic: قَدۡ صَدَّقۡتَ ٱلرُّءۡيَآۚ إِنَّا كَذَٰلِكَ نَجۡزِي ٱلۡمُحۡسِنِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qad saddaqta alrruya inna kathalika najzee almuhsineena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: You have fulfilled the vision." Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: You have already fulfilled the vision.” Indeed, this is how We reward the good-doers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, this was the clear trial.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: That was truly a revealing test.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We ransomed him with a great sacrifice,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We ransomed his son with a great sacrifice,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We left for him [favorable mention] among later generations:
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and blessed Abraham ˹with honourable mention˺ among later generations:

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: "Peace upon Abraham."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: “Peace be upon Abraham.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This is how We reward the good-doers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, he was of Our believing servants.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He was truly one of Our faithful servants.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We gave him good tidings of Isaac, a prophet from among the righteous.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We ˹later˺ gave him good news of Isaac—a prophet, and one of the righteous.

ayahs.textArabic: وَبَٰرَكۡنَا عَلَيۡهِ وَعَلَىٰٓ إِسۡحَٰقَۚ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِهِمَا مُحۡسِنٞ وَظَالِمٞ لِّنَفۡسِهِۦ مُبِينٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wabarakna AAalayhi waAAala ishaqa wamin thurriyyatihima muhsinun wathalimun linafsihi mubeenun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We blessed him and Isaac. But among their descendants is the doer of good and the clearly unjust to himself.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We blessed him and Isaac as well. Some of their descendants did good, while others clearly wronged themselves.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We did certainly confer favor upon Moses and Aaron.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We certainly showed favour to Moses and Aaron,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We saved them and their people from the great affliction,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and delivered them and their people from the great distress.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We supported them so it was they who overcame.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We helped them so it was they who prevailed.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We gave them the explicit Scripture,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We gave them the clear Scripture,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We guided them on the straight path.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and guided them to the Straight Path.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We left for them [favorable mention] among later generations:
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We blessed them ˹with honourable mention˺ among later generations:

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: "Peace upon Moses and Aaron."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: “Peace be upon Moses and Aaron.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, this is how We reward the good-doers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, they were of Our believing servants.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They were truly ˹two˺ of Our faithful servants.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, Elias was from among the messengers,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And Elias was indeed one of the messengers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When he said to his people, "Will you not fear Allah?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Remember˺ when he said to his people, “Will you not fear ˹Allah˺?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Do you call upon Ba'l and leave the best of creators -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do you call upon ˹the idol of˺ Ba’l and abandon the Best of Creators—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Allah, your Lord and the Lord of your first forefathers?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Allah, your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they denied him, so indeed, they will be brought [for punishment],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But they rejected him, so they will certainly be brought ˹for punishment˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except the chosen servants of Allah.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But not the chosen servants of Allah.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We left for him [favorable mention] among later generations:
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We blessed him ˹with honourable mention˺ among later generations:

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: "Peace upon Elias."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: “Peace be upon Elias.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, this is how We reward the good-doers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, he was of Our believing servants.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He was truly one of Our faithful servants.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, Lot was among the messengers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And Lot was indeed one of the messengers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [So mention] when We saved him and his family, all,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Remember˺ when We delivered him and all of his family,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except his wife among those who remained [with the evildoers].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: except an old woman, who was one of the doomed.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We destroyed the others.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then We ˹utterly˺ destroyed the rest.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, you pass by them in the morning
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: You ˹Meccans˺ certainly pass by their ruins day

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And at night. Then will you not use reason?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and night. Will you not then understand?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, Jonah was among the messengers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And Jonah was indeed one of the messengers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Mention] when he ran away to the laden ship.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Remember˺ when he fled to the overloaded ship.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he drew lots and was among the losers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then ˹to save it from sinking,˺ he drew straws ˹with other passengers˺. He lost ˹and was thrown overboard˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then the fish swallowed him, while he was blameworthy.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then the whale engulfed him while he was blameworthy.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And had he not been of those who exalt Allah,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Had he not ˹constantly˺ glorified ˹Allah˺,

ayahs.textArabic: لَلَبِثَ فِي بَطۡنِهِۦٓ إِلَىٰ يَوۡمِ يُبۡعَثُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Lalabitha fee batnihi ila yawmi yubAAathoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He would have remained inside its belly until the Day they are resurrected.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: he would have certainly remained in its belly until the Day of Resurrection.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But We threw him onto the open shore while he was ill.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But We cast him onto the open ˹shore˺, ˹totally˺ worn out,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We caused to grow over him a gourd vine.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and caused a squash plant to grow over him.

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَرۡسَلۡنَٰهُ إِلَىٰ مِاْئَةِ أَلۡفٍ أَوۡ يَزِيدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waarsalnahu ila miati alfin aw yazeedoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We sent him to [his people of] a hundred thousand or more.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We ˹later˺ sent him ˹back˺ to ˹his city of˺ at least one hundred thousand people,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they believed, so We gave them enjoyment [of life] for a time.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: who then believed ˹in him˺, so We allowed them enjoyment for a while.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So inquire of them, [O Muhammad], "Does your Lord have daughters while they have sons?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Ask them ˹O Prophet˺ if your Lord has daughters, while the pagans ˹prefer to˺ have sons.

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ خَلَقۡنَا ٱلۡمَلَٰٓئِكَةَ إِنَٰثٗا وَهُمۡ شَٰهِدُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am khalaqna almalaikata inathan wahum shahidoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or did We create the angels as females while they were witnesses?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or ˹ask them˺ if We created the angels as females right before their eyes.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Unquestionably, it is out of their [invented] falsehood that they say,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Indeed, it is one of their ˹outrageous˺ fabrications to say,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: " Allah has begotten," and indeed, they are liars.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: “Allah has children.” They are simply liars.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Has He chosen daughters over sons?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Has He chosen daughters over sons?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: What is [wrong] with you? How do you make judgement?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: What is the matter with you? How do you judge?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then will you not be reminded?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Will you not then be mindful?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do you have a clear authority?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do you have ˹any˺ compelling proof?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then produce your scripture, if you should be truthful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then bring ˹us˺ your scripture, if what you say is true!

ayahs.textArabic: وَجَعَلُواْ بَيۡنَهُۥ وَبَيۡنَ ٱلۡجِنَّةِ نَسَبٗاۚ وَلَقَدۡ عَلِمَتِ ٱلۡجِنَّةُ إِنَّهُمۡ لَمُحۡضَرُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: WajaAAaloo baynahu wabayna aljinnati nasaban walaqad AAalimati aljinnatu innahum lamuhdaroona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they have claimed between Him and the jinn a lineage, but the jinn have already known that they [who made such claims] will be brought to [punishment].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They have also established a ˹marital˺ relationship between Him and the jinn. Yet the jinn ˹themselves˺ know well that such people will certainly be brought ˹for punishment˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Exalted is Allah above what they describe,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Glorified is Allah far above what they claim!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except the chosen servants of Allah [who do not share in that sin].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But not the chosen servants of Allah.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So indeed, you [disbelievers] and whatever you worship,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Surely you ˹pagans˺ and whatever ˹idols˺ you worship

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: You cannot tempt [anyone] away from Him
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: can never lure ˹anyone˺ away from Him

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except he who is to [enter and] burn in the Hellfire.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: except those ˹destined˺ to burn in Hell.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَا مِنَّآ إِلَّا لَهُۥ مَقَامٞ مَّعۡلُومٞ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama minna illa lahu maqamun maAAloomun
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [The angels say], "There is not among us any except that he has a known position.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹The angels respond,˺ “There is not one of us without an assigned station ˹of worship˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, we are those who line up [for prayer].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We are indeed the ones lined up in ranks ˹for Allah˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, we are those who exalt Allah."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And we are indeed the ones ˹constantly˺ glorifying ˹His praise˺.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And indeed, the disbelievers used to say,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They certainly used to say,

ayahs.textArabic: لَوۡ أَنَّ عِندَنَا ذِكۡرٗا مِّنَ ٱلۡأَوَّلِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Law anna AAindana thikran mina alawwaleena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: "If we had a message from [those of] the former peoples,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: “If only we had a Reminder like ˹those of˺ earlier peoples,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: We would have been the chosen servants of Allah."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: we would have truly been Allah’s devoted servants.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But they disbelieved in it, so they are going to know.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But ˹now˺ they reject it, so they will soon know.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And Our word has already preceded for Our servants, the messengers,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Our Word has already gone forth to Our servants, the messengers,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [That] indeed, they would be those given victory
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: that they would surely be helped,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [that] indeed, Our soldiers will be those who overcome.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and that Our forces will certainly prevail.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So, [O Muhammad], leave them for a time.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So turn away from the deniers for a while ˹O Prophet˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And see [what will befall] them, for they are going to see.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: You will see ˹what will happen to˺ them, and they too will see!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then for Our punishment are they impatient?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do they ˹really˺ wish to hasten Our punishment?

ayahs.textArabic: فَإِذَا نَزَلَ بِسَاحَتِهِمۡ فَسَآءَ صَبَاحُ ٱلۡمُنذَرِينَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faitha nazala bisahatihim fasaa sabahu almunthareena
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But when it descends in their territory, then evil is the morning of those who were warned.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Yet when it descends upon them: how evil will that morning be for those who had been warned!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And leave them for a time.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And turn away from them for a while.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And see, for they are going to see.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: You will see, and they too will see!

ayahs.textArabic: سُبۡحَٰنَ رَبِّكَ رَبِّ ٱلۡعِزَّةِ عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Subhana rabbika rabbi alAAizzati AAamma yasifoona
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Exalted is your Lord, the Lord of might, above what they describe.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Glorified is your Lord—the Lord of Honour and Power—above what they claim!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And peace upon the messengers.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Peace be upon the messengers.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And praise to Allah, Lord of the worlds.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And praise be to Allah—Lord of all worlds.
